title: I'm with the Band

jokes.text: Nick singing Rush's 'The Spirit of Radio' loudly and completely out of key
jokes.setup: Opening with Rush's complex, sophisticated prog-rock song
jokes.punchline: Nick's enthusiastic but terrible singing performance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly establishes Nick's delusional confidence about his musical abilities while being genuinely cringe-worthy

jokes.text: YOU LOOK A LITTLE BIT LIKE MY GRANDPA. OH, IS YOUR GRANDPA SUPER COOL?
jokes.setup: Sam tells someone they look like his grandpa
jokes.punchline: The person assumes this means their grandpa is super cool
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy and realistic teen interaction

jokes.text: JIMMY CARTER'S A WIMP.
jokes.setup: PE teacher mentions President's Physical Fitness Test
jokes.punchline: Student dismisses it because Jimmy Carter is weak
jokes.explanation: Perfect period-appropriate insult that captures teenage political awareness

jokes.text: IS WEIR ALLOWED TO SHOWER WITH US? 'CAUSE HE'S A WOMAN.
jokes.setup: Mandatory shower policy announced
jokes.punchline: Bully suggests Sam is feminine
jokes.explanation: Standard bullying joke but establishes Sam's vulnerability and the shower conflict

jokes.text: WHAT DO I LOOK LIKE, A FREAKIN' TARZAN?
jokes.setup: Student struggling with rope climbing
jokes.punchline: Protests he's not Tarzan
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the absurdity of rope climbing in PE

jokes.text: IT'S NOT A BURLESQUE SHOW, SAM. THEY JUST WANT US TO SHOWER.
jokes.setup: Sam's anxiety about communal showers
jokes.punchline: Neal's matter-of-fact dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal line - logical but missing the emotional point entirely

jokes.text: STOP LOOKIN' UP MY SHORTS. WHY WOULD WE? THERE'S NOTHING TO SEE.
jokes.setup: Student accuses others of looking up his shorts
jokes.punchline: Bill's brutal honesty that there's nothing worth seeing
jokes.explanation: Classic Bill - completely honest and inadvertently devastating

jokes.text: THERE IS SOMETHING TO SEE.
jokes.setup: Bill says there's nothing to see in the student's shorts
jokes.punchline: Student's desperate protest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke showing teenage insecurity

jokes.text: HEY, SCHWEIBER. YOU GONNA SHOW US YOUR CIRCUMCISION IN THE SHOWERS TODAY? YEAH. TAKE A NUMBER.
jokes.setup: Bully tries to embarrass Neal about circumcision
jokes.punchline: Neal's confident comeback suggesting demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal response - turns the insult into confidence, completely unexpected comeback

jokes.text: I CAN'T GO UP ANY FARTHER, GUYS. HOW DO I GET BACK DOWN?
jokes.setup: Bill climbing the rope during their rebellion discussion
jokes.punchline: He's stuck and doesn't know how to get down
jokes.explanation: Classic Bill moment - gets carried away and creates his own problem

jokes.text: The entire Oklahoma! announcement sequence with students' horrified reactions
jokes.setup: Overly enthusiastic drama club member doing announcement
jokes.punchline: Everyone's visible horror and 'OH, NO!' reactions
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about high school theater enthusiasm vs. student apathy

jokes.text: WOW. YOU'D LOOK GOOD IN THAT DRESS. THANKS.
jokes.setup: Daniel commenting on Oklahoma! costumes
jokes.punchline: Lindsay takes it as a genuine compliment
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's earnestness and Daniel's casual teasing style

jokes.text: CREATION. NO, WE DON'T HAVE A NAME.
jokes.setup: Asked about band name, Nick suggests 'Creation'
jokes.punchline: Daniel immediately contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Perfect band dynamic - Nick's enthusiasm vs Daniel's control

jokes.text: HOW ABOUT 3:01? HOW ABOUT 3:15? I GOTTA FIT IN YOUR MOTHER.
jokes.setup: Scheduling rehearsal times
jokes.punchline: Ken's 'your mother' joke
jokes.explanation: Standard 'your mom' joke but fits Ken's crude character

jokes.text: YOU WANNA SLEEP WITH MY MOM? MM-HMM.
jokes.setup: Ken's 'fit in your mother' comment
jokes.punchline: Nick takes it literally, Ken confirms
jokes.explanation: Nick's innocent literalness creates unexpected awkwardness

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, TO BE HONEST, I DON'T SMELL SO GOOD. I COULD PROBABLY USE A SHOWER. NO! WE HAVE TO STICK TOGETHER.
jokes.setup: The geeks planning to avoid showers together
jokes.punchline: Bill admits he needs one, Sam desperately protests unity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bill's honesty vs Sam's desperation

jokes.text: HEY, IF WE DON'T SHOWER, THEN WE ARE GONNA STICK TOGETHER. YOU GET IT?
jokes.setup: Discussion about not showering
jokes.punchline: Neal's literal interpretation of 'stick together'
jokes.explanation: Classic Neal - turns emotional plea into literal observation

jokes.text: WELL...WE DIDN'T EVEN SWEAT. SMELL US.
jokes.setup: Sam trying to avoid shower
jokes.punchline: Offers to let teacher smell them
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward teenage logic and desperation

jokes.text: UH, YOU KNOW, WEIR, I'LL PUT THAT ON MY 'TO DO' LIST.
jokes.setup: Sam offers to let teacher smell them
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect adult sarcasm in response to teen desperation

jokes.text: THAT GUY'S REALLY INTO MAKING GUYS SHOWER. TELL ME ABOUT IT.
jokes.setup: Teacher's insistence on mandatory showers
jokes.punchline: Understated observation about his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Dry observation about the teacher's suspicious enthusiasm

jokes.text: I WISH I COULD SMASH THIS THING. [CHANTING] DO IT! DO IT!
jokes.setup: Nick wanting to destroy his drums after playing
jokes.punchline: Everyone encouraging rock star destruction
jokes.explanation: Captures teenage rock star fantasies vs reality

jokes.text: OH, YEAH? I HOPE IT'S SOMETHING CATCHY, LIKE, UH, THE MATH-LETES.
jokes.setup: Lindsay offering band names
jokes.punchline: Daniel's sarcastic suggestion of Lindsay's math team
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel line - cutting sarcasm that highlights Lindsay's nerd background

jokes.text: MISSION CONTROL. NO WAY. WHY NOT? 'CAUSE IT SOUNDS LIKE SOME SPACE THING. SO? WHAT'S WRONG WITH THAT? SPACE BLOWS.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests 'Mission Control' as band name
jokes.punchline: Ken's definitive 'Space blows' rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with Ken's hilariously blunt dismissal of space

jokes.text: ANARCHY'S CHILD. NO WAY! BOO! BOO BOO BOO. WHAT DOES THAT EVEN MEAN?
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests 'Anarchy's Child'
jokes.punchline: Immediate and dramatic rejection with booing
jokes.explanation: Shows the gap between Lindsay's earnest attempts and the band's taste

jokes.text: I TAKE IT THE SYMPHONY'S OVER.
jokes.setup: Nick's dad arriving after loud, terrible band practice
jokes.punchline: Calling it a 'symphony' sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental sarcasm about terrible teen rock music

jokes.text: [MOCKING] LADIES, GENTLEMEN... LET'S GET OUTTA HERE.
jokes.setup: Nick's dad's formal greeting
jokes.punchline: Daniel mocking the politeness as they leave
jokes.explanation: Shows Daniel's disrespect for authority and adult formality

jokes.text: HEY, YOU WORKING THE DRIVE-THROUGH? YEAH, IN AN HOUR. ALL RIGHT! LET'S GO THERE, GET A BUNCH OF FREE CRAP.
jokes.setup: Planning where to go
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning to exploit Daniel's job for free food
jokes.explanation: Realistic teenage behavior - immediately looking for free stuff

jokes.text: THIS IS MISSION CONTROL REQUESTING PERMISSION TO ROCK OUT.
jokes.setup: Earlier rejection of 'Mission Control' band name
jokes.punchline: Nick using it anyway in a cheesy way
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Nick's obliviousness to why it was rejected

jokes.text: NICK, I REALLY THOUGHT THAT I COULD WALK ON THE MOON, BUT YOU JUST DON'T SEE ANY MOON ROCKS AROUND HERE, DO YA?
jokes.setup: Nick saying he can make it as a drummer
jokes.punchline: Dad's crushing analogy about his own dashed dreams
jokes.explanation: Devastating line that perfectly captures parental disillusionment while being genuinely funny and quotable

jokes.text: Sam's muffled scream from inside the locker
jokes.setup: Sam presumably hiding in locker to avoid showers
jokes.punchline: His muffled distress sounds
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Sam's desperation to avoid the shower situation

jokes.text: THIS EARTH BALL DOESN'T LOOK LIKE THE EARTH. WHOA! WATCH OUT! THAT'S IT. I'M GONNA DIE IN GYM CLASS.
jokes.setup: Using earth ball in PE class
jokes.punchline: Bill's literal observation, then Sam's dramatic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Bill literalness followed by Sam's typical catastrophizing

jokes.text: YEAH, AND I CAN'T SAY NO TO A LONG, LUXURIOUS SHOWER. NO! YOU CAN'T!
jokes.setup: Neal describing desire for shower luxuriously
jokes.punchline: Sam's desperate protest
jokes.explanation: Neal's unexpected sensual description contrasts with Sam's panic

jokes.text: I'M FARSHTINKINER, AND I'M SHOWERING.
jokes.setup: Bill explaining why he's going to shower
jokes.punchline: Made-up Yiddish-sounding word for stinky
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill invention - pseudo-intellectual made-up word that sounds plausible

jokes.text: JUST CLOSE YOUR EYES, AND IT'LL BE OVER BEFORE YOU KNOW IT. I'M WITH HIM. OH, THANKS!
jokes.setup: Bill's advice about showers sounding like other advice
jokes.punchline: Neal agreeing, Sam's sarcastic thanks for abandonment
jokes.explanation: Bill's advice inadvertently sounds like something else entirely

jokes.text: C-COULD YOU PUT ON A TOWEL? WHY? WELL... 'CAUSE YOU'RE NAKED?
jokes.setup: Sam's discomfort with Bill's nudity
jokes.punchline: Bill's genuine confusion about the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Sam's shame and Bill's complete comfort

jokes.text: SAM, OUR BODIES ARE MERELY A SHELL WHICH CONCEAL OUR HEAVENLY SOULS. TRY NOT TO GET TOO UPTIGHT. AUF WIEDERSEHEN.
jokes.setup: Sam's discomfort with nudity
jokes.punchline: Bill's philosophical naked speech ending with German goodbye
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Bill - pretentious philosophy while naked, topped with random German

jokes.text: WELL, YOU COULD STOP SMELLING LIKE MY BUTT, FOR ONE.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what could happen in showers
jokes.punchline: Bill's blunt assessment of someone's smell
jokes.explanation: Classic Bill - completely honest and inappropriately specific

jokes.text: NAIL THE GEEKS! ... OWW! I HOPE I CAN STILL HAVE KIDS. COME ON. YOU GOT HIT IN THE GROIN. YOU DIDN'T HAVE A HYSTERECTOMY.
jokes.setup: Student getting hit and worried about fertility
jokes.punchline: Neal's logical but insensitive medical correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal response - technically correct but missing empathy entirely

jokes.text: LOOK AT THAT HEAD OF HAIR, HUH? YOU JUST GET THAT CUT? THAT IS VERY STYLISH AND VERY, VERY DRY.
jokes.setup: Teacher commenting on Sam's hair
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing it's dry (not wet from shower)
jokes.explanation: Teacher's sarcastic way of pointing out Sam avoided the shower

jokes.text: I'M DIZZY... AND...AND I HAVE A HEADACHE, AND I'M NAUSEOUS.
jokes.setup: Sam claiming to be sick to avoid showering
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic symptoms
jokes.explanation: Standard fake sick routine but shows Sam's desperation

jokes.text: PICK UP THE ACADEMY AWARD ON THE WAY THERE, WEIR.
jokes.setup: Sam's obvious fake illness performance
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sarcastic acting award comment
jokes.explanation: Good teacher sarcasm acknowledging the obvious performance

jokes.text: SCHWEIBER, PUT ON SOME CLOTHES. YOU LOOK LIKE A 50-YEAR-OLD MAN.
jokes.setup: Neal presumably naked or in underwear
jokes.punchline: Teacher's brutal honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Harsh but funny observation about Neal's premature aging appearance

jokes.text: YEAH, BUT YOU GUYS AREN'T GOOD ENOUGH TO WIN ANY COMPETITIONS. THANKS A LOT, YOU KNOW? THANKS A LOT, LINDSAY.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to be helpful about the band
jokes.punchline: Nick's hurt reaction to brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's well-meaning but hurtful honesty

jokes.text: WHEN I WAS IN THE MATHLETES, WE DID LOGARITHMS UNTIL WE COULDN'T SEE STRAIGHT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay giving advice about hard work
jokes.punchline: Comparing rock practice to math competition training
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - applying academic mindset to rock and roll

jokes.text: GEE, THIS IS REALLY FUN, GUYS. I'M GLAD WE HAVE A BAND.
jokes.setup: Tension and fighting during practice
jokes.punchline: Ken's sarcastic comment about how 'fun' it is
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about the band falling apart

jokes.text: WELL, THAT SUCKED. ROYALLY.
jokes.setup: Band finishing their song
jokes.punchline: Ken's blunt assessment with regal emphasis
jokes.explanation: Ken's brutal honesty with comedic emphasis on 'royally'

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, YOU AND YOKO HERE HAVE TURNED MUSIC INTO SCHOOL.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to improve the band
jokes.punchline: Daniel comparing her to Yoko Ono breaking up Beatles
jokes.explanation: Perfect reference that's both cutting and accurate - Lindsay as band-breaking outsider

jokes.text: ROCK 'N' ROLL DON'T COME FROM YOUR BRAIN. IT COMES FROM YOUR CROTCH. AND IF YOU EVER GOT ANY, MR. CUTE SHORTS, YOU'D KNOW THAT.
jokes.setup: Argument about how to approach rock music
jokes.punchline: Daniel's crude philosophy ending with insult about Nick's shorts
jokes.explanation: Classic Daniel - pseudo-profound about rock mixed with personal attack

jokes.text: YOU'RE NOT EVEN GOOD ENOUGH TO BE IN CREATION!
jokes.setup: Band breaking up in anger
jokes.punchline: Nick using the rejected band name as final insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Nick finally asserting the band name in anger

jokes.text: GOOD BODIES. SO HE CAN THROW HER OVER HIS SHOULDER. BIG DEAL. HE SHOULD USE HIS LEGS MORE. HE'S GONNA THROW OUT HIS BACK.
jokes.setup: Watching attractive couple, discussing what they have
jokes.punchline: Bill's practical lifting advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - turns romantic gesture into ergonomic advice

jokes.text: BUT YOU'RE NOT VERY FUNNY. SCREW YOU! I'M HILARIOUS. OOH, HOW WITTY. GET THIS GUY ON HOLLYWOOD SQUARES.
jokes.setup: Discussion about humor attracting women
jokes.punchline: Sam's failed attempt to be funny, Bill's sarcastic game show reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with period-appropriate game show reference

jokes.text: ALL COOL GUYS HAVE HAIRY CHESTS: SELLECK, REYNOLDS, ROCKFORD.
jokes.setup: Discussion about chest hair attracting women
jokes.punchline: Neal's list of hairy-chested icons including TV character
jokes.explanation: Good period references with inadvertent inclusion of fictional character

jokes.text: HEY, WHAT ABOUT MORK? HE'S GOT MORE HAIR ON HIS HAND THAN SAM HAS IN HIS PITS.
jokes.setup: Discussion of hairy-chested cool guys
jokes.punchline: Bill's comparison of alien character's hand hair to Sam's armpit hair
jokes.explanation: Great Bill line - absurd comparison using period TV reference and personal attack

jokes.text: I THINK OUR BEST PLAY IS TO GO FOR THE SMART, SEXY LIBRARIAN TYPE. YOU KNOW, SHE'S SWEET AND SHY, BUT THEN SHE TAKES OFF HER GLASSES AND RRROWF!
jokes.setup: Strategic discussion about which girls might like them
jokes.punchline: Neal's detailed librarian fantasy with growl sound effect
jokes.explanation: Classic teenage male fantasy with Neal's characteristic enthusiasm

jokes.text: LIKE BAILEY ON WKRP IN CINCINNATI. YUP. BUT CINDY SANDERS IS LIKE A LIBRARIAN TYPE. YEAH. LIBRARIAN FOR THE PLAYBOY MANSION.
jokes.setup: Discussing librarian types and Cindy Sanders
jokes.punchline: Neal's reality check about Cindy's league
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the gap between their hopes and reality

jokes.text: Sam running naked through the hallways after being locked out
jokes.setup: Sam's worst fear about communal showers
jokes.punchline: Ends up naked and running through school
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the shower anxiety - his worst nightmare comes true

jokes.text: GOD KNOWS ZEPPELIN ONLY PLAYS HALF OF STAIRWAY TO HEAVEN, AND THE WHO NEVER EVEN PRACTICE TEENAGE WASTELAND. BABBA O'REILLY. WHAT?
jokes.setup: Lindsay's sarcastic music references
jokes.punchline: Daniel correcting the song title, Lindsay's confusion
jokes.explanation: Shows Daniel's real music knowledge vs Lindsay's fake understanding

jokes.text: LINDSAY, HE'S NOT GONNA BE THE NEXT KEITH MOON. SO IF HE'S GOT SOMEPLACE TO GO THAT'S GONNA TAKE CARE OF HIM, WHY DON'T YOU JUST STAY OUT OF HIS WAY?
jokes.setup: Lindsay's idealism about Nick's future
jokes.punchline: Daniel's harsh realism about accepting limitations
jokes.explanation: Dark but realistic perspective that challenges Lindsay's optimism

jokes.text: HEY, YOU KNOW, IT'S A LITTLE EASIER IF YOU SIT DOWN AND DROP YOUR PANTS.
jokes.setup: Sam struggling with something (presumably pants)
jokes.punchline: Teacher's helpful advice that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Innocent advice that sounds wrong out of context

jokes.text: DIMENSION'S LOOKING FOR A NEW DRUMMER?! ... THESE GUYS ARE LIKE THE TOP WORKING BAND IN DETROIT!
jokes.setup: Lindsay showing Nick opportunity
jokes.punchline: Nick's over-excited reaction to mediocre band
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's desperation and low standards for success

jokes.text: I DON'T WANT TO GET NAKED IN FRONT OF OTHER GUYS. WELL, WHO DOES? YOU KNOW HOW MANY MEN HAVE SEEN ME NAKED IN MY LIFETIME? A LOT.
jokes.setup: Sam's shower anxiety
jokes.punchline: Dad's overshare about his own nudity experiences
jokes.explanation: Awkward parental oversharing that doesn't help Sam's anxiety

jokes.text: SAM...YOU HAVE A BEAUTIFUL BODY. DOESN'T HE, HAROLD? YES. I JUST SAID HE HAD A BEAUTIFUL BODY.
jokes.setup: Mom trying to boost Sam's confidence
jokes.punchline: Dad's literal response to redundant question
jokes.explanation: Awkward family dynamics with dad's logical response

jokes.text: YOU'RE AN ADONIS, A SLAB OF BEEF... IF I WASN'T YOUR SISTER, OH, MY GOD-- LINDSAY! CAN IT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay being forced to compliment Sam's body
jokes.punchline: Getting carried away with increasingly inappropriate comparisons
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of awkward family situation going too far

jokes.text: 29. WHOA! 29-PIECE KIT. WOW, WHAT ARE YOU DOING HERE? YOU SHOULD BE AUDITIONING US.
jokes.setup: Nick claiming to have huge drum kit
jokes.punchline: Band member's sarcastic response about who should audition whom
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's transparent attempt to impress backfiring

jokes.text: WELL, YOU MUST'VE BEEN THERE FRIDAY NIGHT, 'CAUSE SATURDAY NIGHT WE GOT BOOED OFF THE STAGE. WE DIDN'T GET BOOED OFF THE STAGE.
jokes.setup: Nick complimenting their Jethro Tull show
jokes.punchline: Band revealing they actually got booed, then defensive correction
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Nick's hero worship with realistic band experience

jokes.text: I HATE THAT SONG. LET'S DO CROSSROADS.
jokes.setup: Nick suggesting 'Sunshine of Your Love'
jokes.punchline: Band dismissing his preferred song
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick can't even pick the song at his own audition

jokes.text: HEY, UH, GOOD LUCK WITH THAT 29-PIECER, MAN. MAYBE SOMEDAY, YOU'LL KNOCK IT UP TO AN EVEN 30.
jokes.setup: Nick's earlier boast about 29-piece drum kit
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic sendoff referencing his lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that drives home Nick's humiliation

jokes.text: SOMETIMES I GO DOWN IN MY BASEMENT, AND I PUT ON A LIVE ALBUM, I CAN... SEE MYSELF ON THE STAGE. ... I'M PETER CRISS OR SOMETHING.
jokes.setup: Nick explaining his rock star fantasies
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of imaginary fame, settling for Kiss drummer
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking and funny glimpse into Nick's delusional fantasies

jokes.text: I'LL BE LUCKY IF I GET TO BE THE GUY WHO PUSHES THE BUTTON THAT MAKES THE RISER GO UP, BUT I'M NOT EVEN GONNA BE THAT GUY.
jokes.setup: Nick's rock star fantasies
jokes.punchline: Cascading realization of his actual limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Nick's devastating self-awareness creeping in

jokes.text: NICE PECS, WEIR. YOU LOOK LIKE A FLAT-CHESTED GIRL.
jokes.setup: Sam finally taking a shower
jokes.punchline: Bully's emasculating comment
jokes.explanation: Generic bullying humor that serves the plot but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: WELL, I'M PROUD OF MY BODY. [MOCKINGLY] OH, HE'S PROUD OF HIS BODY. OH, HE SHOULD BE PROUD. LOOK AT THIS.
jokes.setup: Sam using his parents' advice about being proud
jokes.punchline: Bullies immediately mocking and exploiting his vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of parental advice backfiring spectacularly

jokes.text: STREAKER! GO, SAM! GO!
jokes.setup: Sam running naked through school
jokes.punchline: Students cheering him on as a streaker
jokes.explanation: Turns Sam's humiliation into unexpected hero moment

jokes.text: I JUST WANTED YOU TO KNOW I CAN'T STOP THINKING ABOUT THAT KISS. OH, GOOD. IT REALLY MEANT A LOT TO ME. I'M GLAD.
jokes.setup: Nick's romantic confession
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's polite but clearly uninterested responses
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward romantic mismatch with Lindsay's discomfort

jokes.text: RINGO STARR. YOU FINALLY GOT YOUR WISH, NICK. YOU'RE AS GOOD A DRUMMER AS JOHN BONHAM. OF COURSE, HE'S DEAD.
jokes.setup: Teasing Nick about his drumming
jokes.punchline: Dark joke equating his skill with dead drummer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - technically gives Nick what he wants in worst way

jokes.text: HEY, IS DIMENSION GONNA CHARGE YOU FOR THE PEE STAIN YOU LEFT ON THE DRUM SEAT?
jokes.setup: Nick's humiliating audition
jokes.punchline: Ken's crude question about bodily function during stress
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate way to extend Nick's humiliation

jokes.text: YOU GUYS ARE LOSERS, AND YOU ALWAYS WILL BE. HOW COME YOU'RE THE ONE RIDING HOME WITH NO FRIENDS?
jokes.setup: Alan's generic insult to the geeks
jokes.punchline: Bill's devastating observational comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill comeback - turns the insult around with simple observation

jokes.text: I PLAY SO MUCH BETTER WHEN YOU'RE AROUND.
jokes.setup: Nick playing drums for Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His romantic but inappropriate comment
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's continued romantic delusion about Lindsay

tmdb_overview: Sam, Neal, and Bill befriend a pretty new transfer student, but soon fear of losing her to the popular crowd and try to win her over with a series of fun things they plan for her. Lindsay, Nick, Daniel, and Ken decide to get fake IDs so they can see a hot local band perform at a bar. However, after they go through the trouble of getting their IDs and getting into the bar, the group is stunned to find out who the hot local bands lead singer is.
